title: Hypotheses, Riskiest Assumptions, and Disposable Prototypes · Claim Audit: Risk Priority
summary: A team starts production architecture while desirability, usability, feasibility, viability, safety, and operating assumptions remain untested. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 risk priority increment in ranked assumption map and prototype evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Risk Priority · Theory Risk Priority
steps.instruction:
theory case — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Risk Priority contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Risk Priority · Theory Risk Priority.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence. For Predict Risk Priority · Theory Risk Priority, keep Risk Priority connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

For predict risk priority, apply Risk Priority without its stated preconditions, omit the ranked assumption map and prototype evidence trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Risk Priority for ranked assumption map and prototype evidence.
Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.text: Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Risk Priority while producing ranked assumption map and prototype evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence. A defensible solution applies Risk Priority, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to ranked assumption map and prototype evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: pp1-risk-priority — Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
focus: pp1-risk-priority
decision-evidence: Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

The Risk Priority record says ranked assumption map and prototype evidence looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect risk priority evidence boundary for Risk Priority.

steps.title: Defend the Risk Priority model · Theory Risk Priority
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Risk Priority: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.

Evidence target: Defend the Risk Priority model · Theory Risk Priority.
steps.why: Retrieving Risk Priority through the concrete ranked assumption map and prototype evidence case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the Risk Priority model · Theory Risk Priority, keep Risk Priority connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
